data.settleTest: German colo billing practice: whether tenant power is invoiced at facility-feed/RPP MID-certified meters with PDU metering treated as informational — settleable today via German colo billing specs, MessEG/MID applicability docs, and a handful of operator billing interviews.

proposedCorrection.rationale: If measured-kWh billing in Germany legally requires MID/MessEG-certified meters the entrant lacks, the metered-billing use case in the German half (~50% of funnel) is unreachable in Year 1, lowering the serviceable share from 0.55 toward the low end.

researchQueries.query: German MessEG/MessEV and MID conformity requirements for sub-metering used to invoice colocation tenants on measured kWh — does a PDU-embedded meter require calibration/eichung to produce a legally valid invoice?

German colocation operators (e.g., NTT, Digital Realty, Equinix DE) billing methodology: are tenants billed on facility-feed/RPP certified meters or on breaker-amperage/contracted capacity, with PDU per-outlet metering used only for monitoring?
